title: portuguese zucchini fritters
description: a tasty and kid-friendly portuguese dish featuring zucchini, perfect for a weeknight dinner. the combination of crispy outside and fluffy inside makes it a hit with both kids and adults.
instructions: heat a large frying pan with about 1/2 inch of olive oil over medium-high heat.
slice the zucchini into 1/4-inch thick rounds.
in a large bowl, combine the flour, parmesan cheese, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.
add the eggs to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
add the lemon zest and mix well.
heat the butter until melted, then add to the batter and mix until combined.
dip each zucchini round into the batter, coating both sides evenly.
fry the zucchini rounds until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
drain the fritters on paper towels and serve hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
repeat the process with the remaining zucchini and batter.
serve the fritters hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
optional: serve with a side of garlic mayonnaise or aioli for added flavor.
optional: add some chopped fresh parsley on top of the fritters for extra flavor and texture.
